name: US Cash Advance
description_short: US Cash Advance offers payday loans, cash advances, and bad credit loans up to $50,000 with same-day funding at a Detroit storefront location.
description_long:
US Cash Advance operates a physical lending location in Detroit, Michigan at 20002 Schoolcraft Rd, specializing in rapid-access short-term loans for consumers facing immediate financial needs. The company markets itself as welcoming all credit types, positioning itself as an accessible option for borrowers who may struggle to qualify for traditional bank loans. Their service model combines in-person consultation with stated fast approval timelines, claiming ability to disburse funds within hours of application.

The company offers multiple loan products including payday loans (up to $1,000), cash advances (up to $1,000), bad credit loans ($250-$50,000), same-day loans, and quick personal loans. All products are advertised as unsecured loans requiring no collateral. They emphasize minimum paperwork requirements and acceptance of applicants with bad credit or no credit history. The company states it partners with third-party lenders and conducts standard underwriting review before approval, though specific APR and fee structures are not detailed on the provided website content.

US Cash Advance differentiates itself through in-person service with named staff (notably a representative named Doris cited in reviews) and extended operating hours (8am-9pm weekdays, 10am-7pm weekends). Customer reviews highlight fast processing times (45 minutes to a couple hours) and transparent explanation of terms. The company claims no hidden fees and appeals to borrowers needing funds for medical bills, unexpected expenses, and general cash flow gaps until payday.

The primary caveat is that specific loan terms—APR, repayment periods, and total cost of borrowing—are not disclosed on this landing page, making it impossible to assess whether products comply with payday-alternative standards or carry predatory rates. While reviews are positive, they focus on service speed and staff demeanor rather than loan affordability. The company's positioning as accepting "all credit types" with loans up to $50,000 suggests some products may function as high-cost credit typical of the payday lending industry.
pros: Same-day or next-day funding available (customers report receiving funds within 2 hours)
Accepts all credit types including bad credit with no credit checks required
Physical location in Detroit with extended hours (8am-9pm weekdays, 10am-7pm weekends) for in-person service
Fast in-person processing time (reported 45 minutes average per customer reviews)
Transparent staff communication about loan terms and repayment obligations
Loan amounts available up to $50,000 for bad credit products
cons: Specific APR, interest rates, and total cost of borrowing not disclosed on website—making loan affordability impossible to assess
Repayment terms not specified, limiting ability to understand true cost and obligations
Physical location limited to single Detroit address (20002 Schoolcraft Rd), restricting accessibility
Customer feedback focuses on service speed rather than affordability; no reviews address whether rates are reasonable
Website content incomplete (sitemap cuts off mid-sentence regarding APR and repayment period details)
best_for: Consumers in the Detroit metro area needing immediate cash ($100-$1,000) for urgent expenses
Borrowers with bad credit or limited credit history who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans
People who prefer in-person service and immediate feedback over online-only lenders
diagnosis: US Cash Advance is best suited for Detroit-area residents in immediate financial distress who need $100-$1,000 in 1-2 hours and have poor or no credit history. The primary caveat is complete opacity on loan costs: without disclosed APR, fees, or repayment terms, borrowers cannot determine if this is genuinely affordable emergency credit or a high-cost payday loan that worsens financial hardship. Do not apply without first obtaining written fee and rate disclosures.
